title: Stellaris® RNA FISH 96 Well Glass Bottom Plate Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2qmg1","text":"This protocol is specifically designed for high throughput applications of Stellaris in 96 well glass bottom plates.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dmmm8","text":"Storage Guidelines","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"76vi2","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Probes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3dp0r","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Probes are shipped dry and can be stored at +2 to +8 °C in this state. Dissolved probe mix should be subjected","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"qcm4","text":"to a minimum number of freeze-thaw cycles. For daily and short-term use of dissolved probe mix, storage at +2 to +8 °C in the","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1bsmh","text":"dark for up to a month is recommended. For storage lasting longer than a month, we recommend aliquoting and freezing probes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"92smp","text":"in the dark at -15 to -30 °C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dlcel","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"au3re","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer should be stored at +2 to +8 °C for short-term and long-term use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"85bai","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Wash Buffer A and Wash Buffer B","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4h80l","text":"Stellaris RNA FISH Wash Buffers A and B should be stored at room temperature for short-term and long-term use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

oven","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1g0lj","text":"*Cell culture plate must be resistant to the fixation, wash buffers, and microscope objective immersion oil used in this protocol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"237fi","text":"Microscope:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ahua","text":"a) Wide-field fluorescence microscope (e.g., Nikon Eclipse Ti or equivalent). We provide limited support for confocal applications","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4qin","text":"b) A high numerical aperture (\u003e1.3) and 60-100x oil-immersion objective","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ff2oe","text":"c) Strong light source, such as a mercury or metal-halide lamp (newer LED-based light sources may also be sufficient)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1vunu","text":"d) Filter sets appropriate for the fluorophores","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7lb5d","text":"e) Standard cooled CCD camera, ideally optimized for low-light level imaging rather than speed (13 μm pixel size or less is ideal)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"78b7v","text":"Preparation of Reagents","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8uigb","text":"NOTE: When performing Stellaris RNA FISH, it is imperative to limit RNA degradation. Please ensure that all consumables and","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ememq","text":"reagents are RNase-free. Recipes below are for set volumes. Please adjust accordingly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a3dha","text":"Reconstituting the dried probe stock:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6eiah","text":"ShipReady Probe Set (1 nmol): A ShipReady probe set can provide up to 50 hybridizations. Re-dissolve the dried oligonucleotide","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"29a36","text":"probe blend in 80 μL of TE buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, 1 mM EDTA, pH 8.0) to create a probe stock of 12.5 μM. Mix well by pipetting","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7fnhg","text":"up and down, and then vortex and centrifuge briefly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2unm8","text":"DesignReady or Custom Probe Set (5 nmol): A DesignReady or custom probe set can provide up to 250 hybridizations.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f08s0","text":"Re-dissolve the dried oligonucleotide probe blend in 400 μL of TE buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, 1 mM EDTA, pH 8.0) to create a probe","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f340v","text":"stock of 12.5 μM. Mix well by pipetting up and down, and then vortex and centrifuge briefly.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c52ti","text":"Standard Fixation Solution:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"86it7","text":"Final composition is 3.7% (vol./vol.) formaldehyde in 1X PBS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7suq8","text":"For a final volume of 10 mL, mix:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1eb18","text":"1 mL 37% Formaldehyde solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7a9cg","text":"1 mL 10X Phosphate Buffered Saline (PBS), RNase-free","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eamdh","text":"8 mL Nuclease-free water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"66oor","text":"WARNING! steps: Grow cells in a 96-well glass bottom cell culture plate NOTE: This protocol has been adapted for a 96 well glass bottom system. To adapt this protocol for your preferred system, volumes should be adjusted accordingly.
Decant growth medium, and wash with 200 μL of 1X PBS undetermined
To fix cells, add 200 μL of 3.7% Formaldehyde fixation solution undetermined
Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes 00:10:00
Wash with 200 μL of 1X PBS undetermined
Wash with 200 μL of 1X PBS undetermined
The Alternative Fixation steps are an alternative to the standard fixation and are not meant to be sequential.
Grow cells in a 96-well glass bottom cell culture plate
Decant growth media, and wash with 200 μL of 1X PBS undetermined
To fix and permeabilize cells, add 200 μL of methanol-acetic acid (MeOH-AcOH) fixation solution undetermined
Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes 00:10:00
Cells can be stored at +2 to +8 °C in MeOH-AcOH up to 48 hours before hybridization. Do not use a well if the MeOH-AcOH has completely evaporated

If frozen before using, warm the reconstituted probe stock to room temperature. Mix well by vortexing, then centrifuge briefly. To prepare the Hybridization Buffer containing probe, add 1.5 μL of probe stock solution to 75 μL of Hybridization Buffer, and then vortex and centrifuge (enough for one well). This creates a working probe solution of 250 nM. This solution will be used on step 17. undetermined Stellaris(R) RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-HB1-10
Decant MeOH-AcOH or 70% ethanol from wells containing adherent cells
Add 200 μL of Wash Buffer A (see recipe above), and incubate at room temperature for 2-5 minutes undetermined 00:05:00 Stellaris® RNA FISH Wash Buffer A Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-WA1-60
Decant Wash Buffer A
Add 75 μL of Hybridization Buffer containing Probe into each well undetermined Stellaris(R) RNA FISH Hybridization Buffer Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-HB1-10

Incubate in the dark at 37 °C for 4 to 16 hours a) Incubation is recommended for 16 hours using the Standard Fixation Method b) Incubation is recommended for 2 hours using the Alternative Fixation Method 16:00:00
Aspirate the Hybridization Buffer containing Probe, and add 200 μL of Wash Buffer A undetermined Stellaris® RNA FISH Wash Buffer A Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-WA1-60
Incubate in the dark at 37 °C for 30 minutes 00:30:00

Decant Wash Buffer A, and then add 200 μL of DAPI nuclear stain (Wash Buffer A consisting of 5 ng/mL DAPI) to counterstain the nuclei undetermined Stellaris® RNA FISH Wash Buffer A Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-WA1-60
Incubate in the dark at 37 °C for 30 minutes 00:30:00
Decant DAPI staining buffer, and then add 200 μL of Wash Buffer B. Incubate at room temperature for 2-5 minutes 00:05:00 Stellaris® RNA FISH Wash Buffer B Biosearch Technologies Catalog #SMF-WB1-20
Add 30 μL of VectaShield Mounting Medium to the well and top with 30 μL of Mineral Oil undetermined VECTASHIELD Mounting Medium Vector Laboratories Catalog #H-1000
Proceed to Imaging
